{{ block title }} Part Three {{ endblock }} {{ block content }}

Part three will be very similar to Part two, where we will pay you the average outcome across a very large number of robot buyers. However, the Part Three payments are based on a different version of this experiment. In previous sessions of this study, rather than choosing {{ if single == 1 }} a menu of widgets to offer the Buyer, Sellers instead chose a fixed number of widgets to offer. Where in your session you chose a minimum and maximum quantity to offer, in the other version of this experiment the Sellers offered a fixed number of widgets to the Buyer. Buyers could choose between the offered amount of widgets or to walk away (zero widgets). The Seller's choice was therefore any single widget quantity to offer from 1 to 8. Notice that just as in your session, the Seller could never remove the option for the Buyer to walk away (zero widgets). {{ else }} a fixed number of widgets to offer the Buyer, Sellers instead chose a menu of widgets to offer. Where in your session you chose a single amount to offer, in the other version of this experiment the Sellers offered a minimum and maximum number of widgets to the Buyer. Buyers could choose between any amount of widgets on the offered menu or to walk away (zero widgets). The Seller's choice was therefore both a minimum and a maximum amount of widgets to offer from 1 to 8. Notice that just as in your session, the Seller could never remove the option for the Buyer to walk away (zero widgets). {{ endif }}

On the next page, you will be able to explore the interface that the Sellers and Buyers saw in the alternate version of this experiment.

{{ if test == 1 }} (Next button for testing purposes.)
{{ endif }} {{ endblock }}
